fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Kentucky Physical Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a physical therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Kentucky - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: physical therapist license reciprocity kentucky, how to transfer physical therapist license to kentucky, physical therapist compact kentucky, kentucky physical therapist endorsement application

quick_facts.ce_requirement: 30 contact hours of continuing education every 2 years

license_types.title: PT, DPT
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a physical therapist in Kentucky.

steps.title: Register for an Account
steps.description: Create a new user account on the Kentucky Medical Licensing Portal (kymedical.ky.gov) by clicking 'Register Now' and following the prompts.

steps.title: Complete Online Application
steps.description: Log in to the Kentucky Medical Licensing Portal, select the appropriate Physical Therapist application (e.g., initial licensure by exam or endorsement), and accurately complete all required sections.

steps.title: Pay Application Fee
steps.description: Submit the $150.00 non-refundable application fee electronically through the Kentucky Medical Licensing Portal.

steps.title: Submit Supporting Documents
steps.description: Arrange for all required supporting documents, such as official transcripts, verification of licensure from other states, and National Physical Therapy Examination (NPTE) scores, to be sent directly to the Kentucky Board of Physical Therapy (KBPT).

steps.title: Monitor Application Status
steps.description: Regularly check the status of your application and respond to any requests for additional information through your account on the Kentucky Medical Licensing Portal.

steps.title: Receive License
steps.description: Upon review and approval by the KBPT, your Kentucky PT license will be issued.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination).

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NPTE. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Kentucky physical therapist license cost?
faqs.answer: For endorsement applicants, the state licensing fee is $150. Additional third-party costs (fingerprinting, transcripts, or verification) may apply.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination) is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Kentucky have reciprocity for physical therapists?
faqs.answer: Yes. Kentucky participates in the PT Compact. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Kentucky without a separate endorsement application.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my physical therapist license to Kentucky?
faqs.answer: Current board processing time is 2 to 4 Weeks once your file is complete.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Kentucky while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Physical Therapist in Kentucky
hero.dek: To apply for a Physical Therapist license in Kentucky, navigate to the Kentucky Medical Licensing Portal to register for an account and begin your application.
